summary: DHH observes that Danish TV is saturated with American legal dramas and court shows, to the point that young Danes likely know the US Constitution better than their own. He recommends following a fellow student's example and attending real public court proceedings as a free, educational, and genuinely engaging alternative.
key_points: American legal entertainment dominates Danish television programming
Cultural saturation has left young Danes more familiar with American law than Danish law
Attending real public court sessions offers an authentic and educational experience
money_quotes: Most young Danes would probably be more capable of recalling the letter of the American constitution than that of their own country.
It's free, very real, occasionally touching, and surely educational.
key_insight: The dominance of American legal entertainment has created a cultural literacy gap that could be remedied by engaging with one's own real-world legal system.
